ddls:
CREATE TABLE "vm_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance record */,
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the virtual machine */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the performance data */,
  "time_since_epoch" TEXT /* Time since the Java epoch in milliseconds */,
  "time_since_bag_start_ms" INTEGER /* Time since bag start in milliseconds */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* CPU usage of the VM during the recorded period */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Memory usage of the VM during the recorded period */,
  "network_in" TEXT /* Amount of incoming network traffic in bytes */,
  "network_out" TEXT /* Amount of outgoing network traffic in bytes */,
  "disk_read" TEXT /* Amount of disk read operations in bytes */,
  "disk_write" TEXT /* Amount of disk write operations in bytes */,
  "response_time" REAL /* Average response time for requests processed by the VM in milliseconds */,
  "load_average" TEXT /* Load average on the VM over the last 1, 5, and 15 minutes */,
  "scaling_status" TEXT /* Indicates if the VM is currently being scaled up or down */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vm_performance_vm_id FOREIGN KEY ("vm_id") REFERENCES virtual_machines ("vm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "virtual_machines" (
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each virtual machine */,
  "vm_name" TEXT /* Name of the virtual machine */,
  "data_center" TEXT /* Data center where the VM is hosted */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the VM (e.g., running, stopped) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the VM */,
  "os_type" TEXT /* Type of operating system running on the VM */,
  "os_version" TEXT /* Version of the operating system */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the VM was created */,
  "last_updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the VM's configuration was last updated */,
  "resource_group" TEXT /* Group or category to which the VM belongs for organizational purposes */,
  "is_auto_scale" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the VM is part of an auto-scaling group */,
  "scaling_policy" TEXT /* Policy applied for auto-scaling */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated virtual machine */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., high CPU usage) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Details about the alert */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert */,
  "is_resolved" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the alert has been resolved */,
  "resolved_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was resolved */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the alert */,
  "alert_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_vm_id FOREIGN KEY ("vm_id") REFERENCES virtual_machines ("vm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_centers" (
  "data_center_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data center */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the data center */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Total capacity of resources available in the data center */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Email address for the data center's contact person */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number for the data center's contact person */,
  "maintenance_window" TEXT /* Time window during which maintenance can be performed */,
  "up_time" INTEGER /* Total uptime of the data center in hours */,
  "last_maintenance" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last maintenance performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_center_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, operator) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role and its responsibilities */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was created */,
  "last_updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role's permissions were last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the virtual machine */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date of the report generation */,
  "summary" TEXT /* Summary of the report */,
  "report_file" TEXT /* File path of the report */,
  "generated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  "is_archived" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the report has been archived */,
  "archived_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the report was archived */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_reports_generated_by FOREIGN KEY ("generated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_reports_vm_id FOREIGN KEY ("vm_id") REFERENCES virtual_machines ("vm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_thresholds" (
  "threshold_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each threshold */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of metric (e.g., CPU, Memory) */,
  "threshold_value" REAL /* Threshold value for generating alerts */,
  "alert_duration" INTEGER /* Duration in minutes for which the threshold must be exceeded to trigger an alert */,
  "alert_frequency" INTEGER /* Frequency in minutes at which alerts should be generated */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the threshold was created */,
  "last_updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the threshold was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("threshold_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user-role mapping */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user-role mapping was created */,
  "last_updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user-role mapping was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
